_comment: Curated list of non-functional technical improvement candidates for VirtualRow. Each entry becomes one GitHub issue when the temporary workflow .github/workflows/create-improvement-issues.yml is dispatched. Issues describe what to change and why, but must not alter visible behaviour, simulator output, or test contracts.
_scope: graphic, physics & display — implementation quality only, no functional changes

issues.title: graphic: dedupe per-theme water/mist/bank/sky/atmosphere switch-maps into shared theme config
issues.body:
## Observation
`src/components/Rower3D.tsx` switches on `RouteTheme` in at least seven places — `PhotorealisticWater.waterConfig` (~85 lines), `CurvedWaterChannel.waterConfig` (~65 lines), `CurvedRiverbanks.bankConfig`, `MistLayer.mistConfig`, `ThemedRiverbanks.bankColor`, `getThemeAtmosphere`, `getSkyConfig`, and inline ternaries around `<hemisphereLight>`, `<directionalLight>`, `<ambientLight>` and `<Environment preset=...>` (Rower3D.tsx:478-565, 757-820, 947-1015, 643-700, 2382-2391, 2411-2474, 2492+, 3849-3932).

The `PhotorealisticWater` and `CurvedWaterChannel` water configs in particular contain near-identical numbers and are easy to drift apart.

## Improvement
- Extract a single `themes/<theme>.ts` (or a `THEME_CONFIG` map keyed by `RouteTheme`) that exposes `water`, `mist`, `bank`, `atmosphere`, `sky`, `sun`, `ambient`, `fill`, `envPreset` blocks.
- Replace each `useMemo(() => switch(theme){...})` site with a lookup into the central map.
- No visual change: all current numbers preserved verbatim.

## Acceptance criteria
- All existing unit tests and Playwright tests still pass.
- `Rower3D.tsx` is shorter by several hundred lines, and adding/tweaking a theme touches exactly one file.

issues.title: graphic: extract mirrored left/right oar groups in RowingScullBase into a single <OarRig side="left|right"> component
issues.body:
## Observation
`RowingScullBase` defines the left oar group at `src/components/Rower3D.tsx:~3380-3454` and the right oar group at `src/components/Rower3D.tsx:~3457-3527` — they are mirror duplicates (rigger, oarlock, shaft, grip, blade, blade edge) with sign-flipped X coordinates. Roughly 80 lines duplicated and a known maintenance hazard (drift between sides).

## Improvement
- Introduce `const OarRig: React.FC<{ side: 'left' | 'right' }> = ({ side }) => { const sx = side === 'left' ? -1 : 1; ... }`.
- Replace both groups with `<OarRig side="left" /> <OarRig side="right" />`.
- Preserve materials and dimensions exactly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Snapshot/screenshot test (`playwright/tests/virtualrow.spec.ts`) renders pixel-identical or within an explicit tolerance.
- LOC reduction in `Rower3D.tsx` ≥ 70 lines.

issues.title: graphic: dispose curved water/landscape BufferGeometry on unmount (GPU memory leak)
issues.body:
## Observation
`CurvedRiverbanks` already disposes its left/right `BufferGeometry` on unmount (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:1083-1089`). The very similar geometry in `CurvedWaterChannel` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:843-902`) is **not** disposed when `curve` changes or the component unmounts. The element-position arrays inside `CurvedLandscapeElements.useMemo` (`Rower3D.tsx:1136-1195`) likewise allocate many `THREE.Vector3` objects that survive across route swaps.

Over a session with several route changes this leaks vertex buffers on the GPU.

## Improvement
- Add a cleanup `useEffect` in `CurvedWaterChannel` that calls `waterGeometry?.dispose()` on unmount and on curve change, matching the pattern used by `CurvedRiverbanks`.
- Audit other `useMemo` blocks in `Rower3D.tsx` that build `THREE.BufferGeometry` / `THREE.CatmullRomCurve3` and ensure disposal where appropriate.

## Acceptance criteria
- No behaviour change.
- Manual sanity check: `renderer.info.memory.geometries` does not grow monotonically when switching themes/routes repeatedly.

issues.title: graphic: water plane uses THREE.DoubleSide on 1000×1000 mesh — switch to FrontSide
issues.body:
## Observation
Both `PhotorealisticWater` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:625`) and the curved water mesh use `side={THREE.DoubleSide}` on a 1000×1000 plane with 128×128 tessellation and an expensive `MeshPhysicalMaterial` (transmission, clearcoat, sheen, IOR, Gerstner shader injection). The camera never crosses below water level, so the back side is invisible — yet the renderer pays full fragment-shader cost twice for every fragment.

## Improvement
- Set `side={THREE.FrontSide}` (the Three.js default) on both water materials.
- If a brief edge-case (camera dipping below) is a concern, gate via an explicit camera-Y check or accept; in the current scene the camera Y is clamped above the water.

## Acceptance criteria
- Identical rendering from all camera angles used in the app.
- Fewer fragment-shader invocations on the water surface (measurable via the Three.js inspector or `gl.getQueryParameter`).

issues.title: graphic: reuse scratch Vector3 instances in geometry-builder loops (reduce per-tick GC)
issues.body:
## Observation
Geometry builder `useMemo` blocks construct many short-lived `THREE.Vector3` objects:
- `CurvedWaterChannel` loop: `Rower3D.tsx:862-867`
- `CurvedRiverbanks` loop: `Rower3D.tsx:1037-1045`
- `CurvedLandscapeElements` loop: `Rower3D.tsx:1148-1183`

Each iteration allocates 3–5 `Vector3` (200 segments × 3 sites = ~3000 throwaway vectors per route load). Although these are not per-frame, route swaps trigger noticeable GC pauses on mid-range devices.

## Improvement
- Hoist scratch vectors (`tmpUp`, `tmpPerp`, `tmpPoint`, `tmpTangent`) above the loop and reuse them via `.set(...)` / `.copy(...)`.
- Push primitive numbers directly into the `positions`/`normals` arrays without an intermediate `Vector3`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Identical geometry output (verify by comparing serialised positions).
- Lower allocation profile on a Chrome DevTools allocation timeline during route swap.

issues.title: graphic: throttle/condition water reflection CubeCamera by performanceMode
issues.body:
## Observation
`WaterReflectionProbe` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:445-466`) runs a 64×64 cube-camera render every 30 frames regardless of `performanceMode`. On `performanceMode='low'` it still pays the cost of six face renders ~ twice a second, including all environment/landscape draws.

## Improvement
- Accept the `performanceMode` prop (or read it from a context) and gate the probe:
  - `high` → every 30 frames at 64 resolution (current).
  - `auto` → every 60 frames at 32 resolution.
  - `low` → disable entirely; rely on `<Environment>` HDR only.
- Skip in `__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING` mode (already done at the call site, `Rower3D.tsx:628`).

## Acceptance criteria
- Same visual quality at `high`; measurable frame-time improvement at `low`/`auto`.
- No new props leak through to `PhotorealisticWater` callers (resolve via context).

issues.title: graphic: post-processing chain unconditionally on — gate by performanceMode
issues.body:
## Observation
`DynamicPostFx` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:3581-3611`) always renders the full chain: Bloom + ChromaticAberration + DepthOfField (bokehScale 2) + Vignette + ACES ToneMapping. DepthOfField in particular is the single biggest GPU cost in the scene on integrated GPUs.

The `performanceMode` prop is accepted by `Rower3D` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:181`) but only used to toggle `shadows` and `antialias` at the Canvas level (`Rower3D.tsx:4092, 4146-4150`). It does not influence the post-FX chain at all.

## Improvement
- Pass `performanceMode` into `DynamicPostFx` and render a reduced chain on `low` (e.g. Bloom + ToneMapping only) and skip Bloom entirely on `low` if frame-time still suffers.
- Document the tier matrix in a comment.

## Acceptance criteria
- No visual difference at `high` and `auto`.
- Measurable post-FX cost reduction on `low` (check `renderer.info.render.calls`).

issues.title: display: gate gl.preserveDrawingBuffer behind __PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING
issues.body:
## Observation
The `<Canvas>` is created with `preserveDrawingBuffer: true` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:4152`). This flag forces the browser to keep the back-buffer un-cleared after `present()`, which on most GPU drivers translates to an extra full-frame copy per render. It is only needed because Playwright must screenshot the canvas (see `playwright/tests/virtualrow.spec.ts:1008+` per repository memory).

## Improvement
- Set `preserveDrawingBuffer: !!window.__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING` (or pull from an env-aware constant) so production users do not pay the copy cost.
- Confirm docs-screenshot Playwright test still captures correctly (it sets `__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING = true` before navigation).

## Acceptance criteria
- Playwright docs-screenshot test still produces non-blank `docs/screenshot-rower-3d.png`.
- Lower per-frame GPU cost in production.

issues.title: display: consolidate scattered window.__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING checks into a single module-level constant
issues.body:
## Observation
`Rower3D.tsx` reads `window.__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING` from 9+ sites (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:570, 628, 825, 2855, 3763, 3963, 3984, 3993, 4002`). Each access pays a property lookup and adds branching noise to JSX. The flag also cannot be type-checked centrally — the `// @ts-ignore`-style global typing is duplicated.

## Improvement
- Add at top of file: `const IS_TEST_MODE = typeof window !== 'undefined' && !!window.__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING;` (and a `declare global` block for the typing).
- Replace all 9 sites with `IS_TEST_MODE`.
- Note: this is fine because `__PLAYWRIGHT_TESTING` is set before the SPA bootstraps and never toggles during a session.

## Acceptance criteria
- All existing Playwright tests pass.
- `Rower3D.tsx` typecheck no longer needs scattered `window.` accessors.

issues.title: display: clean up WebGL context-lost/restored listeners on Canvas unmount
issues.body:
## Observation
`Rower3D` wires `webglcontextlost` and `webglcontextrestored` listeners on the GL canvas inside the `onCreated` callback (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:4164-4178`) but never removes them. If the `<Canvas>` remounts (e.g. due to a parent state change, Hot Module Reload, or a `GPUErrorBoundary` retry), the listeners stay registered against the old (now garbage-collected) canvas. In a long session this also keeps stale closures alive that reference an unmounted React tree.

## Improvement
- Move the listener registration into an effect inside a child component (so cleanup can `removeEventListener`), or capture the canvas reference in `onCreated` and register a teardown via `gl.domElement.addEventListener` with a stored handler that the `Canvas` `dispose` callback removes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Retry button on `GPUErrorBoundary` does not leak listeners (verify in Chrome DevTools Performance > Listeners count after several retries).
- Existing Playwright `webglcontextlost`-recovery test continues to pass.

issues.title: physics: usePhysicsEngine setState per frame causes ~60 React re-renders/s — move to refs
issues.body:
## Observation
`usePhysicsEngine.dispatchTick` calls `setBoatState(prev => ({...}))` on every animation frame (`src/hooks/usePhysicsEngine.ts:65`). It is invoked from `RowerScene.useFrame` (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:3683`). React-three-fiber's `useFrame` runs at the display refresh rate (~60 Hz on most monitors, 120+ Hz on high-refresh displays). That means the `RowerScene` component (and any consumer that destructures `boatState`) re-renders on every frame.

The child components (`WakeEffect`, `BladeEntryFoam`, `BoatKinematicController`, post-FX) already consume per-frame values through **refs** to avoid this — so the React-render churn is purely overhead in the parent.

Furthermore, there is a redundant mirror effect at `Rower3D.tsx:3667-3670` that copies `boatState.strokeCycleT` and `boatState.velocityMps` into refs on every re-render — only needed because the state lives in React state instead of refs.

## Improvement
- Convert `BoatState` storage in `usePhysicsEngine` from `useState` to a ref (`boatStateRef`). Expose `getBoatState()` (or return the ref directly) instead of `boatState`.
- Update `dispatchTick` to mutate `boatStateRef.current` in-place (or assign a new object, but skip the `setState`).
- For consumers that genuinely need a re-render on phase change (e.g. `BladeEntryFoam` reads `strokePhase` as a prop), expose a lightweight subscription helper or pass a ref + selector.
- Delete the now-redundant `useEffect` mirror at `Rower3D.tsx:3667-3670`.

## Acceptance criteria
- No behaviour change — boat position, wake, foam, post-FX, and Playwright telemetry (`window.__ROWER3D_*`) all continue to update at the same rate.
- React DevTools Profiler shows `RowerScene` renders drop from ~60/s to a handful per route swap.

issues.title: physics: usePhysicsEngine.workerLatency is dead code — wire it or remove
issues.body:
## Observation
`WorkerLatency` is declared and returned from `usePhysicsEngine` (`src/hooks/usePhysicsEngine.ts:14-21, 51, 81`) with a permanently-zero `DEFAULT_LATENCY`. The comment marks it as a placeholder for a future Wasm physics worker integration that has not landed.

The surface is exposed publicly but has no consumer (`grep -r workerLatency src` returns only the declaration site).

## Improvement
Pick one:
- **Option A (preferred for now):** Remove `WorkerLatency`, `DEFAULT_LATENCY`, and the `workerLatency` return value entirely; document the planned Wasm engine integration separately.
- **Option B:** Keep the type but track real metrics — `lastMs`, rolling `avgMs`, and `p99Ms` of `dispatchTick` itself (still useful even with JS implementation).

## Acceptance criteria
- No public API consumers broken (search confirms no consumers).
- If Option B, add a unit test covering the rolling-window stats.

issues.title: physics: smooth pace→speed conversion to reduce camera/wake jitter
issues.body:
## Observation
`jsSpeedFromPm5` (`src/hooks/usePhysicsEngine.ts:36-39`) computes `speed = 500 / pace` and returns it directly. PM5 pace samples are noisy between strokes (jumps of 10–20 s/500 m within a single stroke cycle are normal). The raw speed drives:
- the boat progress integrator (`Rower3D.tsx:3696-3698`)
- the camera position (camera follows the boat tangent, `Rower3D.tsx:3748-3759`)
- the wake opacity/scale (`WakeEffect`, `Rower3D.tsx:288-299`)
- the chromatic-aberration intensity (`DynamicPostFx`, `Rower3D.tsx:3587-3591`)

So pace noise directly translates into camera/wake/post-FX flicker.

## Improvement
- Apply a one-pole low-pass filter (e.g. `smoothed += (raw - smoothed) * (1 - exp(-dt / tau))` with `tau` ≈ 0.5 s) inside `usePhysicsEngine` before returning the speed.
- Keep the raw value available for telemetry (`window.__ROWER3D_SPEED_MPS`) if existing tests rely on it; otherwise just expose smoothed.
- This is a perception/quality improvement, not a behaviour change: cumulative distance over a workout must remain unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- Existing unit tests in `src/__tests__` pass.
- No regression in Playwright tests that assert telemetry (verify and adjust the test if it asserts on raw instantaneous speed).

issues.title: physics: replace `any`-typed Rapier bodyRef with proper RapierRigidBody type
issues.body:
## Observation
`BoatKinematicController` types the Rapier rigid-body ref as `any` and suppresses lint on the prop type (`src/components/Rower3D.tsx:3547-3550`):
```
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
const bodyRef = useRef<any>(null);
```

`@react-three/rapier` ships a `RapierRigidBody` type for exactly this case.

## Improvement
- `import type { RapierRigidBody } from '@react-three/rapier';`
- `const bodyRef = useRef<RapierRigidBody>(null);`
- Remove the two eslint-disable lines.

## Acceptance criteria
- `npm run lint` and `npm run build` pass.
- No behaviour change.

issues.title: display: split Rower3D.tsx (4188 lines) into per-theme landscape modules
issues.body:
## Observation
`src/components/Rower3D.tsx` is 4188 lines and contains:
- core scene orchestration
- water (straight + curved)
- riverbanks (straight + curved)
- mist + sky
- six themed landscape components (`CrystalBledLandscape`, `GothicVeniceLandscape`, `SteampunkHenleyLandscape`, `DystopianThamesLandscape`, `SciFiBostonLandscape`, `ProceduralTerrain`+`PineTrees`)
- rowing scull + rower humanoid
- post-processing + error boundaries + Canvas wrapper.

This makes HMR slow, file navigation painful, and code review costly.

## Improvement
- Move each themed landscape into `src/components/rower3d/landscapes/<Theme>.tsx`.
- Move `RowingScullBase` + rower humanoid into `src/components/rower3d/RowingScull.tsx`.
- Move water/mist/banks into `src/components/rower3d/water/`.
- Keep `Rower3D.tsx` as the orchestration root only.
- Pure mechanical move — no logic changes.

## Acceptance criteria
- All existing tests pass.
- `Rower3D.tsx` ≤ ~800 lines.
- HMR for a single landscape file no longer rebuilds the rest.

issues.title: graphic: remove unused drei imports (Sky, Cloud)
issues.body:
## Observation
`Rower3D.tsx:3` imports `Sky` and `Cloud` from `@react-three/drei`:
```
import { Environment, Sky, Cloud, useCubeCamera, useGLTF } from '@react-three/drei';
```
Neither `Sky` nor `Cloud` is referenced in the rest of `Rower3D.tsx` (verify with `grep -n '<Sky\|<Cloud' src/components/Rower3D.tsx`).

Drei tree-shakes well, but the unused imports still slightly inflate the dev-time module graph and confuse readers.

## Improvement
- Remove `Sky` and `Cloud` from the import.
- Verify a clean `npm run build` and the bundle analyzer reports no change in production size.

## Acceptance criteria
- `npm run lint` passes with no `unused-import` warning.
- No visual or behaviour change.
